examples_product.input: <description>Login button unresponsive on mobile Safari Users on iOS Safari report that tapping the login button does nothing. No error message is shown. Confirmed on iPhone 13 and 14.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a mobile user on iOS Safari, I want the login button to respond when tapped so that I can access my account.
As a support engineer, I want a clear error state surfaced when login fails so that I can triage user reports faster.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>Tapping the login button on iOS Safari triggers the expected login flow.
If login fails, a visible error message is shown to the user.
The fix is verified on the iPhone 13 and 14 devices mentioned in the report.
No regression is introduced for login on other browsers or platforms.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>Login is a core, blocking function and this report confirms it is broken on a specific platform with no workaround described and no error feedback for the user. Inability to authenticate blocks all downstream usage of the product for affected users, so this warrants a HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Update footer copyright year The footer still shows 2023 instead of 2026. Please update across all pages.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a site visitor, I want to see the correct copyright year in the footer so that the site appears current and trustworthy.
As a marketing stakeholder, I want consistent, accurate footer information across all pages.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The footer copyright year is updated to 2026 on all pages.
The change is verified across the main page templates used site-wide.
No other footer content is altered.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a cosmetic, non-functional text correction with no impact on system behavior, revenue, or user ability to complete tasks. It can be scheduled as routine maintenance, so LOW priority is appropriate.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>ETL pipeline failing nightly load The nightly batch job that loads sales data into the warehouse has failed for the past 3 nights. Downstream dashboards are showing stale data.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a data analyst, I want the nightly ETL job to run successfully so that dashboards reflect current sales data.
As a business stakeholder, I want to trust that reported figures are up to date when making decisions.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The root cause of the nightly load failure is identified and resolved.
The ETL job completes successfully for at least 3 consecutive nightly runs.
Downstream dashboards reflect current data once the fix is deployed.
Alerting is in place to notify the team if the job fails again.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>The pipeline has failed repeatedly over multiple days and is actively causing stale data to be presented to business stakeholders, which can lead to poor decision-making. This is an ongoing data integrity issue affecting reporting, so it should be prioritized as HIGH.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add dark mode toggle Users have requested the ability to switch the dashboard to a dark theme.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want to toggle dark mode on the dashboard so that I can use the product comfortably in low-light environments.
As a product owner, I want to support user preference requests to improve overall satisfaction.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A toggle is available to switch between light and dark themes.
The selected theme persists across sessions.
All dashboard components render correctly and remain legible in dark mode.
The toggle does not affect any existing functionality.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a user-requested enhancement with no indication of urgency, broken functionality, or business risk. It is a quality-of-life improvement rather than something blocking users, so LOW priority is appropriate.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>ML model returning null predictions in production The recommendation model deployed last week is returning null for all prediction requests. This is affecting the recommendations shown to users on the product page.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want to see relevant product recommendations so that I can discover items I am interested in.
As an ML engineer, I want the deployed model to return valid predictions so that the recommendation feature functions as intended.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of the null predictions is identified and resolved.
The model returns valid, non-null predictions for all standard requests.
The fix is verified in production after deployment.
A rollback plan exists in case the issue reoccurs.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>A production ML model is fully failing to return usable output, directly degrading a user-facing feature immediately after a recent deployment. Since this is a current production outage affecting all users of the feature, it should be prioritized as HIGH.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Investigate adding a feature flag service The team has discussed evaluating feature flag tooling for future releases. No immediate need, but worth scoping.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an engineer, I want a feature flag service available so that I can roll out changes incrementally and safely.
As a product owner, I want to evaluate tooling options before committing to a long-term solution.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A short evaluation is conducted of available feature flag tooling options.
Findings and a recommendation are documented.
No implementation is required as part of this ticket.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>The description explicitly states there is no immediate need and frames this as exploratory scoping work for a future release. Without urgency or a defined business driver, this should be prioritized as LOW.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Security vulnerability in authentication library A critical CVE has been published for the authentication library we use in production. Exploit details are publicly available.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a security engineer, I want vulnerable dependencies patched promptly so that the system is not exposed to known exploits.
As a business stakeholder, I want to avoid security incidents that could damage customer trust or violate compliance obligations.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The authentication library is upgraded to a version that resolves the CVE.
The fix is verified in a non-production environment before release.
The production deployment is confirmed to no longer be vulnerable to the published exploit.
The change is communicated to relevant stakeholders.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This involves a critical, publicly known vulnerability with available exploit details in a production authentication system. The combination of severity, public exploitability, and the sensitivity of authentication infrastructure makes this an urgent risk requiring HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Infra cost increase on cloud storage Monthly cloud storage costs have increased by roughly 15% over the last two months. No outage or user impact reported.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an infrastructure engineer, I want to understand the cause of rising storage costs so that we can optimize spend.
As a finance stakeholder, I want cloud costs to remain predictable and aligned with budget.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of the storage cost increase is investigated and documented.
Optimization opportunities, if any, are identified.
A summary of findings is shared with relevant stakeholders.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>There is no reported outage or user-facing impact, so this is not urgent, but a 15% cost increase over two months is a meaningful trend worth investigating before it compounds further. This balance of non-urgency and genuine business relevance supports a MEDIUM priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add export to CSV for analytics report Analysts have asked for the ability to export the weekly analytics report as a CSV file in addition to the current PDF option.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an analyst, I want to export the weekly report as a CSV so that I can perform further analysis in spreadsheet tools.
As a product owner, I want to support common analyst workflows to increase the report's usefulness.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A CSV export option is added alongside the existing PDF export.
The exported CSV contains the same underlying data as the report.
The export completes without errors for standard report sizes.
The existing PDF export continues to function unchanged.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>This is a clear, scoped feature request from a defined user group with practical workflow benefits, but it is an enhancement to existing functionality rather than a fix for something broken. This justifies a MEDIUM priority rather than HIGH or LOW.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Business systems: Salesforce sync delayed Contact records synced from Salesforce to the internal CRM are taking up to 6 hours instead of the expected 15 minutes. Sales team reports working with outdated contact info.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a sales representative, I want contact records to sync promptly so that I am working with current customer information.
As an operations stakeholder, I want the Salesforce integration to meet its expected performance so that downstream teams can rely on it.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of the sync delay is identified.
Sync time is restored to approximately the expected 15-minute window.
The sales team confirms contact records are up to date after the fix.
Monitoring is added to detect future sync delays.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>The sync delay is a significant degradation from expected performance and is already actively impacting the sales team's ability to do their job with accurate data. Because this is causing real operational disruption to a business-critical workflow, it should be prioritized as HIGH.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>AI chatbot occasionally responds in the wrong language A small number of users have reported that the support chatbot sometimes replies in a different language than the one they typed in. Happens intermittently.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want the chatbot to respond in the language I am communicating in so that I can understand and use the support response.
As a support stakeholder, I want consistent chatbot behavior to maintain a quality customer experience.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The conditions under which the chatbot responds in the wrong language are identified.
Language detection or response logic is corrected to match the user's input language.
The fix is validated across the languages supported by the chatbot.
No regression is introduced in chatbot response accuracy.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>This is a real functional defect affecting user experience, but it is described as intermittent and affecting only a small number of users rather than a full outage or critical workflow blocker. This makes MEDIUM priority appropriate, reflecting genuine but limited impact.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add tooltip explaining the 'confidence score' field Users have asked what the confidence score column on the ML results table means. Suggest adding a tooltip with a brief explanation.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want to understand what the confidence score represents so that I can interpret the ML results correctly.
As a product owner, I want to reduce user confusion around technical output fields.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A tooltip is added to the confidence score column header.
The tooltip text clearly explains what the confidence score represents.
The tooltip is accessible via hover or tap depending on platform.
No other table functionality is affected.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a minor usability and documentation improvement that does not affect functionality, data accuracy, or business operations. It can be addressed as routine UI polish, supporting a LOW priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Database connection pool exhausted during peak hours During peak traffic hours, the application server logs show the database connection pool is being exhausted, causing request timeouts for a subset of users.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want the application to remain responsive during peak hours so that my requests do not time out.
As an infrastructure engineer, I want the database connection pool sized and managed appropriately to handle peak load.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of connection pool exhaustion during peak hours is identified.
Connection pool configuration or query patterns are adjusted to prevent exhaustion.
Request timeouts attributable to this issue no longer occur during peak hours.
Monitoring is added to alert on connection pool saturation.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This issue causes request timeouts for users during peak hours, which is when reliability matters most and when the most users are affected. Active, recurring user-facing failures tied to system capacity limits warrant a HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Data warehouse table missing primary key constraint The 'orders' table in the warehouse does not have a primary key constraint, which has allowed duplicate rows to be inserted, inflating revenue totals in reports.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a data engineer, I want proper constraints enforced on warehouse tables so that data integrity is maintained.
As a business stakeholder, I want revenue reports to be accurate and free of duplication.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A primary key constraint is added to the 'orders' table.
Existing duplicate rows are identified and removed or reconciled.
Revenue reports reflect accurate, deduplicated totals after the fix.
A validation check is added to prevent future duplicate insertion.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This issue has already caused inaccurate revenue figures in reports relied upon by business stakeholders, representing a real data integrity and trust problem. Because financial reporting accuracy is directly compromised, this should be prioritized as HIGH.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add additional unit tests for payment retry logic Test coverage for the payment retry logic is currently low. Suggest adding more unit tests to improve confidence in this code path.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an engineer, I want comprehensive test coverage for payment retry logic so that I can make changes with confidence.
As a product owner, I want to reduce the risk of undetected bugs in critical payment flows.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>Additional unit tests are added covering the payment retry logic.
Test coverage for this code path is measurably increased.
All new and existing tests pass in the CI pipeline.
No changes are made to the production retry logic itself.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>This is preventative engineering work on a critical code path involving payments, which carries real business risk if left undertested, but there is no indication of a current defect or incident. The combination of importance without active urgency supports a MEDIUM priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Investigate slow response times on /api/search endpoint Users have reported the search endpoint occasionally takes 8-10 seconds to respond instead of the usual under 1 second. Not consistently reproducible.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want search results to return quickly so that I can find what I am looking for without delay.
As an engineer, I want to identify the cause of intermittent search latency so that performance can be stabilized.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The conditions causing slow response times on the search endpoint are investigated.
A root cause or set of contributing factors is documented.
A fix or mitigation is proposed based on findings.
Search response times are confirmed to be consistently under 1 second after the fix.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>The reported latency spikes are significant and degrade user experience, but the issue is described as intermittent and not consistently reproducible rather than a full outage. This combination of real but inconsistent impact supports a MEDIUM priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Update internal style guide PDF Minor wording updates are needed in the internal engineering style guide PDF. No technical changes required.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an engineer, I want the style guide to reflect current, accurate wording so that I can follow correct conventions.
As a product owner, I want internal documentation to remain up to date.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The specified wording updates are made to the style guide PDF.
The updated PDF is published in its existing location.
No other content in the document is altered.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a minor documentation update with no functional, technical, or business impact, and no urgency is indicated. It can be handled at low priority as routine documentation maintenance.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Production outage: API gateway returning 503 for all requests The API gateway is currently returning 503 errors for all incoming requests across all services. This started 10 minutes ago and is affecting all customers.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want the API gateway to be available so that I can access the product's services.
As an on-call engineer, I want to restore service availability as quickly as possible to minimize customer impact.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The root cause of the 503 errors is identified.
API gateway availability is restored for all services.
All customers are able to successfully make requests after the fix.
A post-incident review is conducted to prevent recurrence.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This describes an active, full production outage affecting all customers and all services simultaneously, which is the most severe category of impact possible. This clearly requires immediate attention and a HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Explore using a vector database for semantic search The ML team wants to explore whether adopting a vector database would improve semantic search relevance. This is a research item for next quarter's roadmap.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an ML engineer, I want to evaluate vector database options so that we can determine if they improve semantic search relevance.
As a product owner, I want to plan roadmap initiatives based on solid technical evaluation.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A research summary comparing vector database options is produced.
Findings include relevance, performance, and cost considerations.
A recommendation is documented for roadmap planning purposes.
No production implementation is required as part of this ticket.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is explicitly framed as exploratory research for a future quarter's roadmap rather than a current need or defect. With no urgency or immediate business driver stated, LOW priority is appropriate.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Inconsistent date formatting across reports Some reports display dates as MM/DD/YYYY while others display DD/MM/YYYY, causing confusion among international stakeholders.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a stakeholder, I want dates to be displayed consistently across all reports so that I can interpret them correctly.
As a product owner, I want to standardize formatting to avoid confusion for our international audience.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A single, consistent date format is defined and applied across all reports.
All existing reports are updated to use the standardized format.
No data values are altered, only their display format.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>Inconsistent date formatting creates genuine risk of misinterpretation for international stakeholders, which could lead to incorrect business decisions, but it is not an outage or broken functionality. This real but moderate risk supports a MEDIUM priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Security: exposed internal API endpoint An internal-only API endpoint was found to be publicly accessible without authentication. It returns internal configuration data.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a security engineer, I want internal endpoints properly restricted so that sensitive configuration data is not exposed externally.
As a business stakeholder, I want to avoid data exposure incidents that could affect compliance or trust.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The exposed endpoint is restricted to authenticated, authorized internal access only.
The fix is verified by confirming the endpoint is no longer publicly accessible.
An assessment is made of whether any data exposure occurred prior to the fix.
Findings are documented for security review.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This describes an active security exposure of internal configuration data with no authentication barrier, which represents a significant and immediate risk. Exposed internal data of this nature requires urgent remediation, so HIGH priority applies.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add additional chart type to analytics dashboard Analysts have requested a stacked bar chart option in addition to the existing line chart for the engagement dashboard.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an analyst, I want a stacked bar chart option so that I can visualize engagement data in a way that better suits my analysis.
As a product owner, I want to support diverse visualization needs for our analytics users.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A stacked bar chart option is added to the engagement dashboard.
Users can switch between the existing line chart and the new stacked bar chart.
The new chart type accurately reflects the underlying engagement data.
The existing line chart functionality remains unaffected.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is an additive visualization feature requested by users with no indication of urgency, broken functionality, or business risk if delayed. As a nice-to-have enhancement, LOW priority is appropriate.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Infra: SSL certificate expiring in 5 days The SSL certificate for the main production domain is set to expire in 5 days. Renewal has not yet been initiated.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want the site to maintain a valid SSL certificate so that my connection remains secure and trusted by my browser.
As an infrastructure engineer, I want certificate renewals handled proactively to avoid service disruption.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The SSL certificate is renewed before the current one expires.
The renewed certificate is deployed to the production domain.
The site is confirmed to serve a valid, non-expired certificate after deployment.
A process is put in place to prevent future last-minute renewals.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>An expiring SSL certificate on the main production domain is a time-bound risk that, if missed, would cause browser security warnings or full site inaccessibility for all users. The short 5-day window before expiry with renewal not yet started makes this HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>ML model accuracy drift detected Weekly monitoring shows the fraud detection model's accuracy has dropped from 94% to 89% over the past month. No production incident reported yet.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an ML engineer, I want to be alerted to and address model accuracy drift so that prediction quality remains reliable.
As a business stakeholder, I want the fraud detection model to maintain high accuracy to minimize financial risk.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of the accuracy drift is investigated.
A plan to restore model accuracy, such as retraining, is proposed.
Model accuracy is confirmed to improve following the fix.
Ongoing monitoring continues to track accuracy over time.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>A 5-point accuracy drop in a fraud detection model represents a meaningful and measurable degradation in a system with direct financial risk implications, even without a reported incident yet. Given the sensitivity of fraud detection and the magnitude of drift observed, this warrants HIGH priority before further degradation occurs.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add keyboard shortcut for save action Users have suggested adding Ctrl+S as a keyboard shortcut for saving within the editor, similar to other tools.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want to use a keyboard shortcut to save my work so that I can work more efficiently.
As a product owner, I want the editor to align with common usability conventions.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>Pressing Ctrl+S triggers the save action within the editor.
The shortcut does not conflict with existing browser or application shortcuts.
The existing save button continues to function unchanged.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a minor usability convenience requested by users with no impact on core functionality or business outcomes if left unaddressed. It fits well as a LOW priority quality-of-life improvement.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Business systems: invoicing tool double-charging customers The automated invoicing system has double-charged at least 12 customers this week due to a bug in the retry logic.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a customer, I want to be charged correctly and only once so that I can trust the billing process.
As a finance stakeholder, I want billing errors corrected immediately to avoid customer harm and reconciliation issues.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The bug in the invoicing retry logic causing double-charges is identified and fixed.
Affected customers are identified and refunded for duplicate charges.
The fix is verified to prevent further double-charging.
A report is provided to finance and customer support stakeholders.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>This issue is actively causing real financial harm to at least 12 customers due to a confirmed bug, which carries both customer trust and financial reconciliation risk. Active billing errors affecting real customers require immediate action and HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Standardize naming convention for new microservices The team would like to agree on and document a naming convention for microservices going forward. No existing services need to be renamed.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an engineer, I want a clear naming convention for microservices so that new services are consistent and easy to identify.
As a product owner, I want to establish good practices early to avoid inconsistency as the system grows.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>A naming convention for microservices is documented.
The convention is shared with the engineering team.
No existing services are renamed as part of this ticket.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>LOW</priority>
<thought>This is a forward-looking process and documentation task with explicitly no impact on existing systems and no urgency indicated. As a planning aid rather than a fix or active need, LOW priority is appropriate.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>AI content moderation flagging false positives at high rate The new content moderation model is flagging roughly 30% of legitimate posts as violations, requiring manual review and frustrating users.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As a user, I want my legitimate posts to not be incorrectly flagged so that I can use the platform without unnecessary friction.
As a moderation team member, I want the false positive rate reduced so that manual review workload is manageable.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The cause of the high false positive rate is investigated.
Model thresholds or logic are adjusted to reduce false positives.
The false positive rate is measurably reduced after the fix.
Legitimate content continues to be appropriately moderated for actual violations.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>HIGH</priority>
<thought>A 30% false positive rate is a severe degradation that directly harms user experience at scale and significantly increases manual review burden on the moderation team. The scale of impact across users and operational workload justifies HIGH priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Add pagination to admin user list The admin user list currently loads all users on one page, which is slow with large user counts. Suggest adding pagination.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an admin, I want the user list to be paginated so that the page loads quickly even with many users.
As a product owner, I want admin tools to remain performant as the user base grows.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>The admin user list is paginated into manageable page sizes.
Page load time is improved for accounts with large user counts.
Admins can navigate between pages of the user list.
Existing search and filter functionality continues to work with pagination.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>This is a real performance and usability issue affecting admin users specifically, with a clear technical cause and fix, but it is limited to an internal admin tool rather than a customer-facing system. This bounded but legitimate impact supports a MEDIUM priority.</thought>

examples_product.input: <description>Infra: container registry approaching storage quota The internal container registry is at 92% of its allocated storage quota. At current growth rate, it will be full within approximately 2 weeks.</description>
examples_product.output:
<user_stories>As an engineer, I want sufficient container registry capacity so that I can continue pushing new image builds without interruption.
As an infrastructure stakeholder, I want storage capacity managed proactively to avoid build pipeline disruptions.</user_stories>
<acceptance_criteria>Additional storage capacity is provisioned for the container registry, or unused images are cleaned up.
Registry usage is brought below a safe threshold of total quota.
A retention or cleanup policy is established to prevent recurrence.
Monitoring or alerting is added for future quota thresholds.</acceptance_criteria>
<priority>MEDIUM</priority>
<thought>This is a real risk with a defined timeline of approximately two weeks before impact, giving the team a reasonable window to act without it being an active emergency yet. The combination of a clear forthcoming impact and some lead time supports a MEDIUM priority rather than HIGH or LOW.</thought>

examples_linking.input:
<ticket1>Login button unresponsive on mobile Safari Users on iOS Safari report that tapping the login button does nothing. Confirmed on iPhone 13 and 14.</ticket1>
<ticket2>iOS Safari login tap not working Multiple users on iPhones report the login button does not respond when tapped in Safari. No errors shown.</ticket2>
examples_linking.output:
<thought>Both tickets describe the exact same symptom (unresponsive login button), the same platform (iOS Safari), and the same lack of error feedback. This strongly suggests they describe the same underlying defect rather than coincidentally similar but unrelated issues.</thought>
<related>True</related>

examples_linking.input:
<ticket1>Footer spelling error The footer text reads 'Suport' instead of 'Support' on the contact page.</ticket1>
<ticket2>ETL pipeline failing nightly load The nightly batch job loading sales data into the warehouse has failed for the past 3 nights.</ticket2>
examples_linking.output:
<thought>One ticket concerns a front-end spelling error and the other concerns a backend data pipeline failure. These involve entirely different systems, symptoms, and root causes with no overlap.</thought>
<related>False</related>

examples_linking.input:

<ticket2>All customer requests failing with 503 errors Customers are reporting 503 errors when calling any API endpoint, started about 10 minutes ago.</ticket2>
examples_linking.output:
<thought>Both tickets report the same error code, the same scope (all services/endpoints), and the same approximate start time, strongly indicating they are reports of the same ongoing outage rather than independent issues.</thought>
<related>True</related>

examples_linking.input:

<ticket2>Admin dashboard 500 error The admin analytics dashboard returns a 500 error when loading the monthly summary view.</ticket2>
examples_linking.output:
<thought>Both tickets describe a 500 error, but they occur on entirely different pages serving different user types, and there is no shared root cause described. The same HTTP error code appearing in two unrelated parts of the system does not indicate a shared underlying issue.</thought>
<related>False</related>

examples_linking.input:
<ticket1>Schema update needed for compliance We need to add a column for model requests and responses for compliance.</ticket1>
<ticket2>Add audit columns to model logging table Add two new columns to track model input and output for audit/compliance purposes.</ticket2>
examples_linking.output:

<related>True</related>

examples_linking.input:
<ticket1>Search endpoint slow response times The /api/search endpoint occasionally takes 8-10 seconds to respond instead of under 1 second.</ticket1>
<ticket2>Recommendations endpoint slow The /api/recommendations endpoint has been responding slowly, taking 6-8 seconds during peak hours.</ticket2>

The matching symptom (slow response time) alone is not sufficient to establish they share a root cause.</thought>
<related>False</related>

examples_linking.input:
<ticket1>SSL certificate expiring in 5 days The SSL certificate for the main production domain expires in 5 days and renewal has not started.</ticket1>
